sentences.text: I keep hearing… I keep hearing that COVID's going around again, so….

No!
No.

Hey, Mark.
Bye!

Good!
Has Blake… he's not joined, has he?

Perfect.
Yes, Blake is here.
… Yes, there is.
Okay.

Happy Wednesday.

I don't know, have you met.
Yep, we've met.
Okay, lovely.
And then, we have Andre on the call as well, who's our product specialist.
And then, I'm Shelby, Nice to meet you.
Hi, SP.
Okay, well, my understanding is the purpose of today's call is to go ahead and run Blake through the social AI tool as well as the dashboard, is that correct?
I don't know about the dash… I don't even know what the dashboard is, so maybe, yeah, because I don't know what it is.
Okay, okay, great.
Lovely.
Well then, yes, I guess I will go ahead and recap, what I heard from Mackenzie.
Just as far as y'all's social strategy currently.
So my understanding is right now, on-site teams are supposed to be posting to Facebook, ideally 3 times a week, but, you know.
of course, everything… everyone has things on their plates.
Sometimes they're not able to get around to that.
And also, as far as, like.
the contact… or the content's a little redundant.
Is that correct?
Just, you know, as far as, like, diversified content?
Facebook, social media, Swiping, right?
Not Facebook Marketplace?
Yes, Facebook, yeah, Facebook profiles, yep.
Okay.
Yeah, I think that's the marketing plan.
They're supposed to do that a few times a week, yes.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Well then, yes, I will go ahead and, hop into the deck, show you how it works.
We can also play around in the dashboard.
Mackenzie, was there anything in particular that you wanted me to speak to, or the team to speak to?
You know, so I gave Blake just kind of a very quick, … rundown of what we had discussed about the automation and the scheduling, right?
And he had some questions, and I don't think that we covered it, or I don't know if that's something that the tool does, is, you know.

Blake had mentioned, we only have so many likes and followers, so how can we utilize the tool to ensure that we're capturing more, versus only showing these great videos that you have to offer, I'm sorry, great posts, just to who we have already?
I am so sorry.
Are you… you guys were with Renable?
Yes, yes, yeah, exactly.
I've seen Mavin AI, or I must say all this stuff.
Okay, so you guys are rentable, we're currently using, you guys are posting, or we're using the social media ads already, $400 additional for select properties to be… to have scrolling.
People are scrolling, our properties will pop up, right?
And then, of course, also, it's about reputation management, I think it's something different that we have.
So then, Mac, I mentioned you guys can do auto-posts for us on our personal pages, … or stories, posts, one of the… one of the two, right?
… And then, yeah, so my concern was… Most of the people that are following us are already our residents or, like, our vendors.
Right?

And so, when, we're currently posting on social media based on Max and kind of my direction, it's more geared towards Hey, we have referral programs going on, we have this event going on, you know, whatever, because there's not a lot of, … I don't think there's a lot of, productivity that would come from, hey, we have a 3x2 available, and here's what it looks like, or here's the price, or whatever, because we're sending that to people that already live with us.
Yep.
Besides the word-of-mouth part, right?
So then McKenzie brought us that you could post for us automatically 3 times a week.
My question was, why?
Yeah.
Yeah, no, we… we can definitely speak to that.
Yeah, so, I mean….
I mean?
I guess as far as y'all's posting currently, your initiative to post 3 times a week.
As far as, like, that goes, was it just to, you know, keep the profiles up to date, or what was the reasoning, kind of, behind, hoping that the teams posted 3 times a week?
That's a great question.
Probably for more resident engagement than for leasing.
There's….
Perfect.
add to that?
Yeah.
Facebook's algorithm… Does allow for people, for them to not have to like us or follow us for it to still show up, right?
Yeah.
Where Instagram does work differently in that sense.
So we have kind of been by the general rule of, on Instagram, we're posting to current residents, and keeping that focus.
On Facebook, we're keeping it more so specific to what we have to offer, but we don't list price or things like that, just so we don't, you know, get the hater comments.
But what we do is, on Marketplace, whenever we're posting something, we try to match that on the property page as well, so that way, if somebody does come to that page.
But yes, to Blake's point, like, we're not… we're not getting a lot of followers from it.
Yeah.
to be sure.
growing.
up, we're not… it's not then leading to them… Liking the business page.
Okay, so ideally, y'all are hoping for these residents to then go and like the… the company, the corporate page, is that correct, or…?
business page, and then that way, with the algorithm, the traction, it will show up in others' feeds of people who are friends with those people, right?
Like, it keeps trickling down.
Yeah, so with Google, so y'all have our Google Business Profile Automation tool, that's of course going to be more geared toward prospective renters.
So, we want to make sure that we're able to really paint.
your property in a good light, so when prospective residents are looking, they're able to gather as much information from your Google Business profile.

But with Facebook and Instagram, it's a lot more geared towards the residents, and Carly, Andre, please correct me if I'm wrong, but as far as a strategy for Meta, that's more geared, ideally, toward your residents.

So while we, of course, are able to automate that content and make sure that we are highlighting your properties in a great light for when those prospective residents do stumble across your pages, your teams are also then able to post about those community events that you're having as well.
So….
jump in real, real briefly.
So, I think another thing that's changed recently that I think makes it more important to, have, like, a regularly updated social media feed is that Google's now indexing, meta posts.
And they're bringing that into Google search, so individual posts can be indexed on Google and show up as search results.
But even more important than that is that that's now within the body of knowledge that Google uses when they're looking for generative search signals.
And so, like, the way we kind of split our content that we automate the feed for, like, on social.

some of it is kind of towards prospective, or, like, current residents, like tenant appreciation, or… and obviously the ability to manually, post things through our tool, so you could do, like, event hostings and things like that.
But then the flip side of it is that, we also… we get that same content about.
like amenities and different features that are available on the property, we get that into another location that then is then indexed by Google.

So if you imagine, like, hey, we're filling up the Google profile, we're filling up the Facebook profile, we're filling up the Instagram profile, now we have multiple of these signals that are saying.

hey, this is a nice apartment with a pool in this area, and so if you imagine somebody, like, you know, increasingly people are doing generative search, AI mode search is a real big thing on Google now, so that increases the chance that if somebody is asking a question about that, or doing a search query that's asking about, like, looking for apartments with certain amenities or things like that, that just means Google now has more fresh signals.
that say, like, hey, this is current information about this apartment, so I'm more likely to answer with this specific apartment.

And then the last thing I'd say is just that, like, one… and we don't have a ton of data to back this up, but, like, one thing that I would say is anecdotally true in my life and, like, in my friend's life is that you know, when you're looking at, like, a business or, like, an apartment, you might just check out the Instagram page, like, briefly, you know?
And one of the benefits we see of our tool is that, with our tool, you are guaranteed that every time somebody does that.

there's fresh content on the page, and you don't have the circumstance of, like, oh, I go there, and it's like, I haven't posted, like, in 3 months, you know, or 2 months, or whatever it might be, you know?
And so, like, that's the other benefit we see on the prospective renter side.
Now, obviously, it's hard to track that, exactly how that converts to leads, you know?
But we do think, just like the Google business profile, where increasingly people are.

especially younger people, they're maybe less likely to go to a website in order to kind of, like, check things out, and they're more likely to stay in the Google Maps app, or look in Instagram, or something like that.
So that's the other way we see it feeding into, like, the perspective area.
So it's kind of search optimization, this kind of, like, quick check that people might do on properties, like, those are the ways that it benefits prospective renters.

And then just keeping the profiles alive, I think, we think has, you know, general benefits, saves time on the team, so they can focus more on these, like, specific things that can only be posted manually.
Yeah, so that's kind of the benefits that we see from the automation.
Cop.
Okay.
Perfect!

Let me go ahead and share my screen.

So…

kind of as we touched on a moment ago, similar to how we, create content on Google, we're gonna do the same thing on Facebook and Instagram via integration with your property management software, to, create that automated content.
So.
With social, the way that it works is, we will make sure to highlight the most engaging amenities.

as well.
We've found that the most, highly, trafficked days on Facebook and Instagram are gonna be Tuesdays and Thursdays, so those are the days that we do automate or create that automated content.
As far as what those automated posts are about, those are gonna be, community and in-unit amenities.
We do, again, as Andre mentioned.
Prospective residents.
We'll take a peek at these profiles, so we do have a schedule tour posts, we like to highlight pets and holidays, so on and so forth.

So, even these automated content that we are posting, we still want to make sure that it is geared toward the property as much as possible.
Yeah, and then just a quick preview, this is going to be coming, in the next couple of weeks, and again, this kind of goes more towards, the current renter community.
I think it's beneficial for prospective renters as well.
We're going to be launching, local posts, so automated local posts, so, to start with, we'll be indexing for every location what are, kind of attractive restaurants nearby that location.
once a month, we'll make a post about, something.
We'll talk about how close it is, and, use an image that's relevant to that particular type of cuisine.

I'm going to be getting onboarded, so….

photos as well, rather than just, like, generalized content.

Gives you an opportunity to edit anything that's already been scheduled, kind of adjust anything, alert us to anything you want to see us, like.
improve in our processes.
And yeah, so you can see pretty far, into the future, both for Google and for social, and you see them all in one place.

So, you'll be able to see both the upcoming As well as the published content all in one place, as well.
But then going back to creating and hosting that content, let's say that movie night that y'all are having, you can select as many properties as you want, you select where you want this to go out.

tools support videos, so you'll be able to post reels to Instagram and regular videos to Facebook.

And then… Yeah, I think that's it.

look at them.

I don't have any data on that.
I know that… I know some of the… some of the, you know, operators we work with Who are, like, really advanced marketing-wise, they seem to do it.

I could see how it would, but….

I would just be curious, like, if you put hashtag luxury living, or hashtag luxury Apartments Fresno, or whatever in those posts, if those would then end up somewhere else besides whoever's following us.

Yeah, and I would say that generally what we see is that most people are doing kind of the latter of what you said, which is so, like, going narrow enough with the hashtag that it's, like, market-specific.
Rather than just, like, luxury living, because that throws you into this pool that's so big.
And I would say, like, a lot of our customers do kind of like what you're saying, like, luxury living Fresno, or something like that, you know?
And… but again, sorry, I don't have any data on it, but I think it… A lot of people do it, so I think it's probably beneficial, but that's a good… I need to look into that, actually.
I should do some research on that.
I would say, from just my own in social media, I think I've noticed that rather than using hashtags.
That might be.
sort of a way of the past.
I'm not 100% sure, again, just my own knowledge.

I think the consistency is what drives followers a little bit more than the hashtags, just being a little bit more consistent in how often you're posting, and that goes for, like, all different social media outlets, like TikTok, Instagram.
So I think that's something to keep in mind as well.
I mean, it definitely can't hurt, but I'm just saying it's important to look at both.

Class A, B, C, I mean, I have some Class D properties on there as well that do utilize, the tool, or even if not us, they are just sure to have, yeah, their own Facebook and Instagram profiles as well.
Yeah, I think social media is becoming a place that a lot of people go to look first, even sometimes before Google.

knowledge, but….

200 photos, some kind of crazy amount of content, you know?

are being sued.
